key_insight: OrchRM trains a reward model on orchestration-level win-lose comparisons from intermediate multi-agent artifacts, enabling self-supervised evaluation and better coordination without human labels.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to improve multi-agent orchestrator training and test-time scaling with much lower token cost, making LLM-based MAS more efficient to deploy.
method: Build win-lose pairs from intermediate artifacts of multi-agent runs
Train a Bradley-Terry reward model to score orchestration quality
Use the reward model to guide orchestrator training and test-time scaling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports up to 10x token savings during training and up to 8% higher accuracy in multi-agent test-time scaling across math reasoning, web QA, and multi-hop reasoning.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of extracted intermediate artifacts and the reward model may not generalize equally well across all agent architectures or task types.
